meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in town patrol and law pressure

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Iron shutters, old brick, courthouse-town texture, and mining-commerce streets.

image_prompt.subject: a night watchman with a shotgun and smoky lamp walking past shuttered brick ruins and timber storefronts while a dog watches an alley
image_prompt.scene: cold moonlit street after rain; dim lamp circles, damp boards, unreadable wanted sheets, a jail door key ring at the belt, mountain dark behind town, and one upstairs curtain slightly open
image_prompt.composition: square nocturne with the lamp circle as the only warm center; alleys press close, the watchman is off-center, and the dog sees what he might not
image_prompt.camera: third-person trailing view like a stealth patrol in an early-HD western game, low enough to catch mud and lamp reflection
material_cues: key ring on worn leather belt
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let the image ask whether he is protecting the town or protecting what the town did. Keep his gun low and the shadows busier than he is.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine stealth patrol scene
render_traits: slightly simplified watchman, dog, and storefront silhouettes
modest texture resolution on lamp glass, wet brick, wood, mud, and leather
hard ambient occlusion in alleys, under awnings, and behind shutters
strong warm lamp against cold moonlight
believable 1899 town patrol gear and buildings
quiet suspicion instead of action pose
style_modifiers.palette: lamp amber, wet brick red, mud black, moon gray, shutter iron, dark pine, shotgun brown, and weak window gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable wanted posters, signs, labels, or text. Papers should be indistinct period shapes.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The watchman, lamp, shotgun, dog, alley, and wet street are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 town patrol life.
The mood is practical and suspicious, not theatrical or modern.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
